title: EMBODIED MANIFESTATIONS of SPACE (CalArts doctoral research)
thesis_claim: Space in this practice is produced through recursive feedback among bodies, architectures, and technological systems rather than treated as a neutral container.
cybernetic_loop: Visitor action perturbs the system, system state reorients perception, and human response re-enters the loop as second-order observation.
material_system: Dissertation corpus + research context writing + installation documentation functioning as operational theory.
agency_model: Reflexive co-observation: artist and machine are mutually observing participants in a shared control ecology.

evidence_refs.label: Research Context Draft
evidence_refs.excerpt: I argue that space is produced through feedback between bodies, architectures, and technological systems, not merely a neutral container.

evidence_refs.label: Tub Proposal Research Context
evidence_refs.excerpt: Across recent works I have treated technological systems as performers with their own limited but persistent forms of memory, temperament, and refusal.

evidence_refs.label: Primary Dissertation Corpus
evidence_refs.excerpt: Primary theoretical corpus used for the seminar's cybernetics segment and conceptual frame of second-order observation.

risk_notes: Theoretical density can outpace audience pacing without concrete project anchoring.
